content.text:
Lets try to wrap up week 6. Refer @docs/progress.md , WEEK6_TODO.md and for the cloud components check the HARNESS_TODO.md. 
Also lets ship DataQ to cloud.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reviewing the three tracking documents to understand what's left for Week 6 and the cloud deployment.

content.content:
1	# Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting · working TODO
2	
3	> **Local scratch — gitignored** (`*_TODO.md`). Source of truth stays [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) + epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175). This file is the *execution plan*: ordering, file pointers, acceptance, commit boundaries. Update freely; don't commit.
4	
5	**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.
6	**Scope:** 27 tasks (ADR [0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)). Full 13-screen prototype set as dedicated pages — **Share is the only drawer; prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page / layout conflict.** Chart lib = **recharts** (lazy-loaded). Currently 2/27 (Results scaffold + severity colours, PR-C1).
7	
8	**Working rules (CONTRIBUTING):** one functionality per commit · manually test before next · feature branch off `main` · conventional commits · `/code-review` agents before merge · run format/lint/typecheck/bandit/tests locally before push · fixup commits on open PRs (no amend+force) · file defects as issues (`Fixes #N`) with the W6 milestone.
9	
10	**Prototype source:** project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via `DesignSync` (`method=get_file path=templates/app/<Screen>.jsx`). `readme.md` = canonical spec. Build in real **antd** reading `theme.ts` — the `.jsx` is reference only.

92	- [ ] Add env, datasource-type, suite, date-range filters to `/results` (status + provider already shipped PR-C1)

102	## Phase 4 — Drawer → page restructures (prototype wins)

# Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting · working TODO

> **Local scratch — gitignored** (`*_TODO.md`). Source of truth stays [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) + epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175). This file is the *execution plan*: ordering, file pointers, acceptance, commit boundaries. Update freely; don't commit.

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.
**Scope:** 27 tasks (ADR [0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)). Full 13-screen prototype set as dedicated pages — **Share is the only drawer; prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page / layout conflict.** Chart lib = **recharts** (lazy-loaded). Currently 2/27 (Results scaffold + severity colours, PR-C1).

**Working rules (CONTRIBUTING):** one functionality per commit · manually test before next · feature branch off `main` · conventional commits · `/code-review` agents before merge · run format/lint/typecheck/bandit/tests locally before push · fixup commits on open PRs (no amend+force) · file defects as issues (`Fixes #N`) with the W6 milestone.

**Prototype source:** project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via `DesignSync` (`method=get_file path=templates/app/<Screen>.jsx`). `readme.md` = canonical spec. Build in real **antd** reading `theme.ts` — the `.jsx` is reference only.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundation (unblocks everything)

### 0.1 Install recharts + chart wrapper  ▸ commit `feat(frontend): add recharts + lazy chart primitives` ✅ `255bf49`
- [x] `pnpm add recharts` (3.8.1) in `frontend/`
- [x] `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` → clean (no known vulnerabilities)
- [x] `components/charts/chartTheme.ts` (status→hex maps + axis/grid/tooltip tokens from theme.ts) + `ResponsiveChart.tsx` (one height/empty contract; sole recharts import point → stays out of initial bundle)
- [x] 4 unit tests for colour maps; format/lint/typecheck/test green (222/222)
- [x] _Done in Phase 2.3 (`b600447`):_ recharts was in the eager `vendor` chunk → split into its own lazy `recharts` chunk (vendor 388→42 kB); ships only with the /dashboard route

- [x] Registered now: `/dashboard`, `/results/:runId`, `/settings` (admin-gated shell). **Deferred to their phases (no throwaway stubs):** `/suites/new` → 4.1; `/connections/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → 4.2 (land with their real pages)

- [x] Back→Results · status/checks-passed/triggered-by/started/duration stat cards · per-check results table (check + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed via `ScalarValue`)
- [x] Row click in `RunsTab` → `navigate(/results/:runId)`; deep-linkable + refreshable (fetch by id via `getRun`)
- [x] **Sample failing rows stay withheld** (PII, [#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226)); 3 RunDetail tests + Results test updated to assert navigation

- [x] CSV: check, expectation, status, metric, observed (RFC-4180 escaping); JSON: run meta + checks array; filename = suite slug + short run id; disabled when no results
- [x] _Ref:_ `DownloadMenu` in `ResultsScreen.jsx`

1	# DataQ — Live test/demo-data environment · local tracker
2	
3	> **NOT git-tracked** (matched by `.gitignore` → `*_TODO.md`). This is the working tracker for the
4	> external harness described in **ADR 0021** (`docs/adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md`).
5	> The harness code itself (Terraform, mock-data generators, Databricks notebook) also lives **outside**
6	> the DataQ repo — keep paths/links here pointing at wherever you stand that up.
7	>
8	> Purpose: discharge the standing *"deferred live warehouse/file run smoke"* caveat with a real,
9	> repeatable retail data environment exercising all three DataQ run paths + the ADF/Airflow trigger path.
10	>
11	> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[-]` skipped/deferred
12	
13	Suggested order: **0 → 1 → 2 → (3a/3b/3c in parallel) → 4 → 5 → 6** (perf rides §2 volume + §3 run paths).
14	Harness root: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` · secrets: `secrets.sh` (chmod 600, never committed)
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 0. Prerequisites (accounts + local tooling)
19	
20	- [x] Azure account/subscription enabled; resource group `dataq-rg` created in West US 2 (sub: `Azure subscription 1`)
21	- [x] Snowflake account enabled — `PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383` (AWS us-west-2, Standard); `snow connection test` OK; creds in `secrets.sh` + `~/.snowflake/connections.toml` (chmod 600)
22	- [x] Databricks workspace enabled — express setup (AWS us-east-2); Unity Catalog metastore `metastore_aws_us_east_2` auto-provisioned; PAT `dapi...` in `secrets.sh`; host `https://dbc-b16e0901-d85c.cloud.databricks.com`; workspace admin confirmed via SCIM API
23	- [x] Local tooling: `terraform` 1.15.7, `az` CLI 2.87.0, `snowflake-cli` (`snow` 3.21.0), Docker — all installed
24	- [x] Secret handling decided: `secrets.sh` in harness root (chmod 600, sourced before terraform/snow commands, never committed); Terraform SP `dataq-terraform-sp` created with Contributor on `dataq-rg`
25	
26	---
27	
28	## 1. Terraform — cloud infra bootstrap (external, not git-tracked)
29	
30	> Provisions the **datasources** (ADLS / Snowflake / UC) **and the compute platform**:
31	> a shared **Azure Container Apps Environment** hosting (a) **Airflow** (decision 2026-06-26 —
32	> on Container Apps, *not* local Docker, *not* ADF Managed Airflow which Microsoft retired for
33	> new instances on 2026-01-01) and (b) the **mockdata file-creation Job** (§2a), plus a
34	> **container registry** for both images and a small **Postgres** for Airflow metadata.
35	> ADF is used in its managed cloud form (its own Schedule/Storage-Event triggers).
36	> **Cloud Airflow unlocks real Event-Grid push** for the file-landing feeds (a laptop behind
37	> NAT couldn't receive it) — so event triggers can be true push, not just poll-sensors.

54	
55	## 2. Retail data model + mock-data generators (external harness)
56	
57	> Domains → entities to generate. Believable retail data so checks/profiling/severity/trends look real.
58	>
59	> **Status: built — `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/mockdata/` (Python package, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib, no Faker).** `python -m mockdata --tier S --seed 42 --out ./out`. Verified tier-S run: 14 datasets, referential integrity holds, 11 deliberate issues land exactly per the manifest (ruff+black clean). Emits to a local `out/` tree shaped like the landing zone; ADLS upload / warehouse load is §3.
60	
61	- [x] **Product** — sku_id, style_id, category, colour, material, size, unit_price, active, created_at (`products`)
62	- [x] **Inventory** — on_hand, planned, daily snapshot with `load_ts` (`inventory_snapshot`, Parquet per date)
63	- [x] **Location** — store, address, open/close hours (`locations`)
64	- [x] **Orders** — `orders_header` (order_number, channel, customer, status, subtotal, tax, order_total, ts) ↔ `order_lines` (sku, qty, unit_price, line_total). `subtotal = sum(line_total)` tie-out shaped clean; intra-dataset reconciliation target #1
65	- [x] **Sales lifecycle** — placed · shipped · audited events carrying `order_number` (`sales_events`, Flow B bronze; rolls up to gold revenue for the cross-platform reconcile)
66	- [~] **Payments / Finance** — `payments` (method, status, captured_amount) + `refunds` (amount, reason); `order_total ↔ payment_captured ↔ refund` testbed live. **Settlement ledger not modelled** (defer until a check needs it)
67	- [~] **Return / Cancellation** — `returns` table (order, sku, reason, channel, qty) + `cancelled` order status. Cancellation is a status, not a separate entity (sufficient for v1)
68	- [~] **Logistics** — shipment/tracking/carrier via `tracking_events` (stable `tracking_number` across ready→in_transit→delivered batches). **Fulfilling node not modelled** (optional)
69	- [x] **Channel / Marketplace** — online · store · marketplace as a first-class `channels` dimension + `channel` FK on orders/returns/feedback (null-channel + orphan issues seeded)
70	- [x] **Guest / Customer** — `customers` (name, address, **email, phone** PII for #226) + `feedback` survey (rating, comment, email). Chatbot folded into feedback
71	- [~] **Cross-cutting** — `promotions` (type/value/coupon-style) + per-order `tax`. **Exchange rate / standalone discount not modelled** (USD-only; add if a check needs them)
72	- [-] _Optional (domain realism, lower priority — add if a check needs them) — deferred:_
73	  - **Supplier / Vendor + Purchase Orders** — inbound supply → inventory replenishment (freshness on inbound feeds)
74	  - **Fulfillment node / DC / warehouse** — the location that fulfilled an order (a join target beyond the store `Location`)
75	  - **Catalog hierarchy** — category · department · brand above Product (orphan / FK-integrity checks)
76	  - **Calendar / date dimension** — fiscal week · season for trend slicing
77	
78	**Route map — which dataset lands on which DataQ run path** (decided; per-flow detail in §3):
79	
80	| Dataset | Route | Platform · format / layer |
81	|---|---|---|
82	| Logistics **tracking events** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **CSV** · batch-resolved (latest) |
83	| **Inventory daily snapshot** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **Parquet** · latest-snapshot |
84	| **Orders** (header+line) · **Payments/Finance** · **Returns/Cancellations** · **Customer** master · **Product/SKU** master | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Snowflake** (SQL-heavy: custom-SQL, line-grain, reconciliation) |
85	| **Location** · **Promotions/pricing/coupons** · **Channel/Marketplace** | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Unity Catalog** (plain load — proves the UC runner *without* medallion) |
86	| **Feedback/survey/chatbot** · **Sales → revenue marts** | Flow B — medallion | **Databricks** bronze → silver → gold (UC) |
87	
88	> **Discipline — one system-of-record per entity.** Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` revenue is a *derived* aggregate that must reconcile **back** to it (Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` — the ADR 0014 `comparison` demo). Never author the same entity authoritatively in two places, or the reconciliation / FK checks measure generation noise instead of real DQ — the same guard as the key-consistency rule above.
89	>
90	> **Reserved-kind homes:** medallion **gold** tables host freshness/volume/anomaly monitors (ADR 0012 — daily aggregates that should refresh); the flat-file feeds host freshness + batch-resolution; the Snowflake↔Databricks pair hosts the cross-platform `comparison`.
91	
92	- [x] Generator script (Python, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib — **no Faker**, stdlib `random`+pools for referential control) emitting CSV/Parquet/NDJSON, laid out per the route map. **Emits to local `out/`** (ADLS upload is §3; the folder tree is already landing-zone-shaped)
93	- [x] **Keep shared keys consistent across domains AND across batch/incremental loads** — shared key-pool strategy in `mockdata/generate.py` (masters generated first, transactional tables draw from the pools). **Verified:**
94	  - `order_number` identical across **Orders · Sales · Return · Logistics · Payments · Refunds**
95	  - `sku_id` identical across **Product · Inventory · Orders · Returns**
96	  - one `tracking_number` stable across `ready_for_shipment → in_transit → delivered` across the 3 batch files (verified: identical set across all batches, 639 shipments tier-S)
97	  - _Verified clean:_ subtotal==sum(line_total) 733/800 (the 67 = deliberate dup-line injection), orphans/nulls exactly per manifest — the deliberate issues are the **only** inconsistencies.
98	- [x] **Shape ≥1 dataset for each reserved monitor kind** (ADR 0012) — done in the generator (built post-v1, shaped now):
99	  - **Freshness · Volume · Anomaly** — `inventory_snapshot` (timestamped `load_ts`, N daily batches; one snapshot thinned to ~30% for volume/anomaly; latest `load_ts` pushed +26h for a freshness fail)
100	  - **Schema drift** — newest `inventory_snapshot` renames `on_hand → qty_on_hand`
101	  - **Comparison / reconciliation** — `sum(line_total)+tax == order_total` (intra) + `order_total ↔ payment.captured ↔ refund` (cross-table) + `order_total ↔ gold.daily_revenue` (cross-platform via `sales_events`)
102	- [x] **Seed deliberate DQ issues** — nulls · dupes · FK orphans (`SKU-000000`/`CUST-000000`) · stale freshness · volume drop · schema drift · reconciliation mismatch. All recorded in `out/_dq_issues_manifest.json` with affected keys (the QA/QE agent §4 asserts failures land *exactly* there)
103	- [x] Parameterise volume (`--tier S/M/L/XL`, perf §6) + `--seed`; `--no-issues` for clean data; run command + full docs in `mockdata/README.md`

106	
107	## 2a. Recurring cadence — incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)
108	
109	> The §2 generator is **one-shot** (backfills N snapshots in a single run). Freshness/
110	> volume/anomaly monitors only mean something if data keeps *arriving* on a cadence.
111	> **Three clocks — do not conflate:** (1) **data-arrival** (source produces new data —
112	> NEW, this section); (2) **load+trigger** (ADF/Airflow picks up the file → triggers
113	> DataQ — §3); (3) **check-run** (DataQ's W5 `schedules` cron, or driven by clock 2).
114	>
115	> **Decisions (2026-06-26) — two layers, don't collapse them:**
116	> - **File creation (clock 1, source-system simulator) = Container Apps Job on a cron schedule.**
117	>   Runs the `mockdata` image (`tick → write files to ADLS landing zone`). Chosen over a
118	>   Function App (pandas/pyarrow is a heavy *batch*, not lightweight event glue — Functions
119	>   add packaging/cold-start friction) and over OS cron; reuses DataQ's Container Apps platform
120	>   + image, cron built in, pay-per-run. **This is *upstream* of the orchestrator** (source drops
121	>   files), which is cleaner/more realistic than the orchestrator also producing.
122	> - **Load + trigger (clock 2) = ADF/Airflow** load to Snowflake/UC → on-success trigger DataQ
123	>   (the path under test). **Deliberately mix two trigger modalities — not all event-driven:**
124	>     - **Event / file-landing** (ADF Storage-Event trigger / Airflow file-sensor or dataset DAG) —
125	>       fires when the Job drops a file. Use for high-frequency/latency-sensitive feeds (e.g. orders).
126	>     - **Schedule / time** (ADF Schedule or tumbling-window trigger / Airflow cron `schedule`) —
127	>       fires on a clock, processes whatever landed in the window. Use for daily/batch feeds
128	>       (e.g. inventory, feedback). **Set the load schedule to fire *after* the file-creation Job**
129	>       (e.g. Job drops inventory 01:00 → scheduled load 01:15) so there's data to pick up.
130	>     - _Why both:_ exercises both ADF/Airflow trigger types **and** DataQ's pipeline-run monitoring +
131	>       10-min polling / gap-recovery fallback (the poll only meaningfully bites on scheduled pipelines).
132	>   Flow C (flat-file in place) has no load step — the Job's file drop *is* the arrival; DataQ reads
133	>   in place (its own W5 schedule or arrival event).
134	>   Proposed split (adjustable): **orders → event**, **tracking → event**, **inventory → schedule**,
135	>   **feedback → schedule** — one event + one schedule per warehouse target (Snowflake & UC) for parity.
136	> - **Required by the Job runtime:** Container Apps Jobs are **ephemeral** → the generator's
137	>   state must move off local disk to **blob** (ADLS). `state.py` needs a pluggable backend
138	>   (local FS today; ADLS blob for the Job). See work items below.
139	
140	- [x] **Generator `tick` mode** — `python -m mockdata tick --feed {orders,tracking,inventory,feedback}` emits **one** batch-suffixed file per dataset (`<name>_b000123.<fmt>`, append-style), not the full backfill. `init` subcommand seeds state + emits the reference loads once. (`mockdata/increment.py` + `state.py`)
141	- [x] **Persisted state** (`state/state.json` + `state/masters/*.parquet`): master pools + climbing id counters (`order_number` never collides) + open-shipment registry (a `tracking_number` from an orders tick gets `ready→in_transit→delivered` on successive tracking ticks). **Verified:** 2 order ticks → ranges 1–40 / 41–80, 0 collisions; tracking# identical across the 3 lifecycle batches; cross-feed FK integrity holds; ruff+black clean
142	- [x] **Per-feed cadence map** (cron) — documented in `mockdata/README.md`: orders `0 0,6,12,18 * * *` · tracking `0 0,12 * * *` · inventory `0 1 * * *` · feedback/reference `0 1 */2 * *`; masters via `init` (rare). Anomaly flags `--volume-drop`/`--stale`/`--schema-drift` + `--issues` per tick
143	- [x] **`state.py` pluggable backend** — `StateBackend` seam: `LocalStateBackend` (default) + `AdlsStateBackend` (Gen2 blob via `DefaultAzureCredential`, azure libs lazy-imported). `make_backend()` picks by URI (`adls://<acct>/<fs>/<prefix>` else local path). **Verified:** local tick flow intact post-refactor; ruff+black clean. ADLS impl coded, runnable against real ADLS post-apply
144	- [x] **`Dockerfile`** (`python:3.13-slim` + pandas/pyarrow/azure; `ENTRYPOINT python -m mockdata`) + `.dockerignore` (keeps secrets.sh/terraform/out/state out of context). **Verified:** image builds; in-container `init`+`tick` write the route layout; **state survives across separate container runs** (mounted vol here; ADLS backend in the Job); `azure SDK OK` in image
145	- [x] **emit output backend → ADLS** — factored the byte-storage primitive into a shared `storage.py` `Store` seam (`LocalStore` + `AdlsStore` + `make_store`, used by **both** `state.py` and `emit.py` — one ADLS-auth impl, no dup). `emit.py` routes every write (csv/parquet/json via buffers + jsonl `append_text`) through the store; `--out adls://<acct>/<fs>[/<prefix>]` now writes the landing zone directly. **Verified:** local backfill+tick intact; ruff+black clean; in the image, `make_store` routing + lazy azure import + DataLake client construct, and a write reaches the Azure auth layer (fails only on missing identity — managed identity supplies it in the Job). **Job is now fully standalone** (no mount needed); only live ADLS write awaits apply
146	- [~] **Container Apps Job (cron)** per feed — `mockdata_job.tf` (`azurerm_container_app_job` × `var.feed_crons`, `schedule_trigger_config` + `tick --feed <name>` → `--out/--state adls://…`); shares the §1 Environment + registry; ADLS write via the storage connection string (`AdlsStore` reads `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` — no role assignment). Authored, validate-clean, apply-pending (build+push image first)
147	- [~] **ADF/Airflow load+trigger** (§3) — **mix event + schedule triggers**: schedule via Airflow (`flow_a_snowflake_load` authored + parses, more next), event via ADF (pipelines next); `load` (Snowflake/UC) → `on_success` → DataQ trigger binding. Schedule loads offset *after* the file-creation Job. Flow C = no load. Live runs apply-gated
148	- [ ] Verify a few cadence ticks produce **monotonic** freshness/volume series in DataQ `/results` (the monitors trend, not just one green dot)
149

152	## 3. Reference flows (each exercises a distinct DataQ run path)
153	
154	### 3a. Flow A — load to warehouse  →  `SnowflakeCheckRunner` / `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`
155	
156	> **Datasets:** **Snowflake** ← Orders (header+line) · Payments/Finance · Returns/Cancellations · Customer master · Product/SKU master (transactional + financial core; custom-SQL + line-grain + `sum(line_total)=order_total` + `order_total↔payment↔refund` reconciliation). **Unity Catalog (plain load)** ← Location · Promotions/pricing/coupons · Channel/Marketplace (governed reference data — proves the UC runner *without* the medallion machinery).
157	
158	> **Harness Airflow lives in `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/airflow/`** (image `Dockerfile` + `requirements.txt` + `dags/`). Image = `apache/airflow:2.10.5` + key-based client libs (snowflake-connector, databricks-sql, azure-datalake) + DAGs; DataQ trigger via the copied `dataq_airflow_callback.py`. All loads = whole-subdir overwrite (idempotent; per-file watermark = follow-up).
159	
160	- [x] File lands in ADLS landing zone (from §2 generator) — via the §2a mockdata cron Jobs
161	- [x] **Orchestrator picks up + loads** — **Airflow** (schedule): `flow_a_snowflake_load` (7 feeds → Snowflake, `write_pandas`) + `flow_a_uc_reference` (3 feeds → UC `reference`, databricks-sql). **ADF** (event): `terraform/adf_pipeline.tf` — storage-event trigger → Copy ADLS→Snowflake → Web-activity DataQ webhook. **Verified: all DAGs DagBag-parse clean in the image (0 errors); ADF TF validate-clean.** Live load apply-gated
162	- [x] On success, the orchestrator hits DataQ's trigger path — Airflow `on_dataq_success` (HMAC → `/events/airflow`); ADF Web activity POST (`token=` shared secret → `/events/adf`, body `factoryName/pipelineName/runId/status`). Lands live once DataQ URL reachable
163	- [~] DataQ suite runs against the loaded **warehouse table**; result row persists — suites + trigger-bindings specced in `dataq_setup/` (`flow_a_snowflake`, `flow_a_uc_reference`); live run apply+deploy-gated
164	- [ ] Verify in `/results`: run succeeded, severity derived, metric_value populated — apply+deploy-gated
165	- [~] **Snowflake suite** exercises a **custom-SQL** check (ADR 0019) — `order_total <> ROUND(SUM(line_total)*1.08,2)` reconciliation specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`; create at deploy
166	- [~] **UC plain-load suite** on Location/Promotions — `flow_a_uc_reference` suite specced in `dataq_setup/` (no Silver/Gold — that's Flow B)
167	
168	### 3b. Flow B — medallion  →  UC tables
169	
170	> **Datasets:** **Feedback/survey/chatbot** (bronze raw JSON/text → silver cleaned + **PII-handled** [#226] → gold sentiment/NPS feature table — the lakehouse/ML "feature store" story) and **Sales → revenue marts** (bronze raw sales events → silver cleaned daily sales by store·channel → gold revenue/units rollups).
171	
172	- [x] File lands in bronze — generator drops `databricks_medallion/bronze/{sales_events,feedback}/*.json`; notebook reads them into UC `raw`
173	- [x] **Databricks notebook** runs bronze → silver → gold — `databricks/medallion_notebook.py` (key-based ADLS via spark conf; raw load → silver clean+PII-hash → gold daily_revenue rollup). Submitted by `flow_b_medallion` DAG via the Databricks SDK (parses clean in the image)
174	- [~] DataQ suites on UC Silver/Gold — `flow_b_silver`/`flow_b_gold` specced in `dataq_setup/`; live apply+deploy-gated
175	- [x] **Gold hosts the reserved freshness/volume/anomaly targets** — `gold.daily_revenue` (daily rollup) is the shaped target
176	- [x] **Cross-platform reconciliation target** (ADR 0014) — Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` (sales_events `placed.amount` = order_total → rolls up to tie out); the `comparison` demo shaped
177	- [x] notebook completion triggers DataQ — `flow_b_medallion` DAG `on_dataq_success` → `(airflow, flow_b_medallion)` binding
178	- [ ] Verify results per layer (bronze schema-drift, silver PII-redaction, gold freshness/volume) — apply+deploy-gated
179	
180	### 3c. Flow C — flat-file in place  →  `FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution
181	
182	> **Datasets:** **Logistics tracking events** (ADLS · **CSV** · event-append — `tracking_number` stable across `Ready For Shipment → In Transit → Delivered` batches) and **Inventory daily snapshot** (ADLS · **Parquet** · daily-snapshot — exercises the zero-copy Arrow read, PR #140). One of each format × each batch shape (event-append vs daily-snapshot).
183	
184	- [x] File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) — `adls_flatfile/{logistics_tracking,inventory_snapshot}/`, no orchestrator load step
185	- [x] DataQ flat-file connection + suite targets the path with a **batching regex** — specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`: tracking `tracking_events_(.+)\.csv`, inventory `inventory_snapshot_(.+)\.parquet`, strategy `latest` (first capture group = batch key). No trigger-binding — runs on DataQ's own schedule
186	- [ ] Run resolves the **latest** batch and checks it directly — apply+deploy-gated
187	- [ ] Verify batch resolution picks the right file + result persists — apply+deploy-gated
188	- [~] **CSV** path: tracking events — checks specced (tracking-number/event-status + freshness) in `dataq_setup/`
189	- [~] **Parquet** path: inventory snapshot — checks specced (volume band ±20% + freshness on latest) in `dataq_setup/`

193	## 4. QA/QE agent — module-by-module + E2E (this IS git-tracked: `.claude/agents/`)
194	
195	> The one in-repo deliverable. A Claude Code subagent, sibling to `migration-safety` /
196	> `orchestration-abstraction-guard`. Lands in Week 8 / ongoing.
197	
198	- [ ] Define agent scope: per-module functional checks (connections, suites, checks, run paths, profiler, results, orchestration) + full E2E against the harness
199	- [ ] Write `.claude/agents/qa-qe.md` (tools, model, system prompt)
200	- [ ] Wire it to drive the three flows above as E2E smoke once the harness is up
201	- [ ] Decide invocation cadence (per-PR? on-demand? pre-release?)
202	- [ ] **This file is committed** — open a PR per working agreements (branch `feature/qa-qe-agent`)

206	## 5. HashiCorp Vault — secret-management spike (post-v1 / backlog)
207	
208	> Validates the `SecretStore` seam (ADR 0010/0013): Azure Key Vault is **one** impl, not the architecture.
209	
210	- [ ] Stand up Vault locally (dev mode / Docker) in the harness
211	- [ ] Prototype a `VaultSecretStore` against the existing `SecretStore` interface (spike branch, not merged)
212	- [ ] Confirm no interface change needed (if it needs one, that's a finding worth an ADR note)
213	- [ ] Write up: does the seam hold? effort to make Vault a first-class option? → backlog issue / ADR follow-up
214	
215	---
216	
217	## 6. Performance / scale testing (post-v1 / backlog)
218	
219	> Rides §2's parameterizable volume (`--seed` + a `--volume` knob): generate **S / M / L / XL** of the same
220	> datasets, run, measure, record. **Baseline-first** — establish numbers + a regression budget; no hard SLAs
221	> yet (set them once we know where the knees are). Standalone harness script; the **QA/QE agent (§4)** can
222	> invoke it later as a perf-smoke. Needs §2 (generators) + §3 (the run paths) green first.
223	
224	**Lead axes (do these first):**
225	- [ ] **Run scaling** *(headline)* — GX run wall-clock vs **rows × #checks-per-suite**, per datasource (Snowflake · UC · flat-file CSV+Parquet). Capture throughput (rows/sec) + **worker memory peak** (GX pulls the batch in-memory) — and note whether time is in the warehouse query or GX itself.
226	- [ ] **Profiler latency** — profile a **wide** table (many columns) to quantify the known **N+1 per-column top-values** cost ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)) and the payoff once it's batched.
227	- [ ] **DB growth** — p95 latency of `/results`, `/dashboard/summary`, `/runs` as `results`/`runs` grow to 100k+ rows; validates the retention-sweep index ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)).
228	
229	**Secondary axes:**
230	- [ ] **Concurrency / throughput** — N suites at once: Celery pool saturation, the 60s scheduler beat under many due schedules (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), run-now storms → queue wait time.
231	- [ ] **Flat-file batch resolution at scale** — resolving the latest batch among a large landing zone (thousands of files) — the list+regex cost.
232	
233	**Method:**
234	- [ ] One script: param = datasource × volume tier × #checks; emits a CSV/JSON of (metric, value) per run
235	- [ ] Record a **baseline table** (commit it with the QA/QE agent in §4) + a **regression budget** (e.g. fail if a tier regresses > X% vs baseline) — so perf regressions are caught, not just measured once
236	- [ ] Capture both **app** metrics (run duration, profiler latency, API p95) and **resource** metrics (worker mem peak, DB CPU)
237	- [ ] Feed findings back: confirm/close #327 (profiler) + #323 (retention index); file new issues for any knee found
238	
239	---

243	- [ ] File the milestoned GitHub issues (per convention): Terraform → W7 · mock-data + Flows A/B/C → W7 · QA/QE agent → W8 · Vault → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)" · perf/scale testing → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)"  *(currently NOT filed — pending decision)*
244	- [ ] When a flow first runs green end-to-end, update `docs/progress.md` (flip the ⬜ in the workstream table) and note the live smoke is discharged in CLAUDE.md §13

> Domains → entities to generate. Believable retail data so checks/profiling/severity/trends look real.
>
> **Status: built — `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/mockdata/` (Python package, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib, no Faker).** `python -m mockdata --tier S --seed 42 --out ./out`. Verified tier-S run: 14 datasets, referential integrity holds, 11 deliberate issues land exactly per the manifest (ruff+black clean). Emits to a local `out/` tree shaped like the landing zone; ADLS upload / warehouse load is §3.

- [x] **Product** — sku_id, style_id, category, colour, material, size, unit_price, active, created_at (`products`)

- [~] **Return / Cancellation** — `returns` table (order, sku, reason, channel, qty) + `cancelled` order status. Cancellation is a status, not a separate entity (sufficient for v1)
- [~] **Logistics** — shipment/tracking/carrier via `tracking_events` (stable `tracking_number` across ready→in_transit→delivered batches). **Fulfilling node not modelled** (optional)
- [x] **Channel / Marketplace** — online · store · marketplace as a first-class `channels` dimension + `channel` FK on orders/returns/feedback (null-channel + orphan issues seeded)
- [x] **Guest / Customer** — `customers` (name, address, **email, phone** PII for #226) + `feedback` survey (rating, comment, email). Chatbot folded into feedback
- [~] **Cross-cutting** — `promotions` (type/value/coupon-style) + per-order `tax`. **Exchange rate / standalone discount not modelled** (USD-only; add if a check needs them)
- [-] _Optional (domain realism, lower priority — add if a check needs them) — deferred:_
  - **Supplier / Vendor + Purchase Orders** — inbound supply → inventory replenishment (freshness on inbound feeds)
  - **Fulfillment node / DC / warehouse** — the location that fulfilled an order (a join target beyond the store `Location`)
  - **Catalog hierarchy** — category · department · brand above Product (orphan / FK-integrity checks)
  - **Calendar / date dimension** — fiscal week · season for trend slicing

**Route map — which dataset lands on which DataQ run path** (decided; per-flow detail in §3):

| Dataset | Route | Platform · format / layer |
|---|---|---|
| Logistics **tracking events** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **CSV** · batch-resolved (latest) |
| **Inventory daily snapshot** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **Parquet** · latest-snapshot |
| **Orders** (header+line) · **Payments/Finance** · **Returns/Cancellations** · **Customer** master · **Product/SKU** master | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Snowflake** (SQL-heavy: custom-SQL, line-grain, reconciliation) |
| **Location** · **Promotions/pricing/coupons** · **Channel/Marketplace** | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Unity Catalog** (plain load — proves the UC runner *without* medallion) |
| **Feedback/survey/chatbot** · **Sales → revenue marts** | Flow B — medallion | **Databricks** bronze → silver → gold (UC) |

> **Discipline — one system-of-record per entity.** Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` revenue is a *derived* aggregate that must reconcile **back** to it (Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` — the ADR 0014 `comparison` demo). Never author the same entity authoritatively in two places, or the reconciliation / FK checks measure generation noise instead of real DQ — the same guard as the key-consistency rule above.
>
> **Reserved-kind homes:** medallion **gold** tables host freshness/volume/anomaly monitors (ADR 0012 — daily aggregates that should refresh); the flat-file feeds host freshness + batch-resolution; the Snowflake↔Databricks pair hosts the cross-platform `comparison`.

## 2a. Recurring cadence — incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)

> The §2 generator is **one-shot** (backfills N snapshots in a single run). Freshness/
> volume/anomaly monitors only mean something if data keeps *arriving* on a cadence.
> **Three clocks — do not conflate:** (1) **data-arrival** (source produces new data —
> NEW, this section); (2) **load+trigger** (ADF/Airflow picks up the file → triggers
> DataQ — §3); (3) **check-run** (DataQ's W5 `schedules` cron, or driven by clock 2).
>
> **Decisions (2026-06-26) — two layers, don't collapse them:**
> - **File creation (clock 1, source-system simulator) = Container Apps Job on a cron schedule.**
>   Runs the `mockdata` image (`tick → write files to ADLS landing zone`). Chosen over a
>   Function App (pandas/pyarrow is a heavy *batch*, not lightweight event glue — Functions
>   add packaging/cold-start friction) and over OS cron; reuses DataQ's Container Apps platform
>   + image, cron built in, pay-per-run. **This is *upstream* of the orchestrator** (source drops
>   files), which is cleaner/more realistic than the orchestrator also producing.
> - **Load + trigger (clock 2) = ADF/Airflow** load to Snowflake/UC → on-success trigger DataQ
>   (the path under test). **Deliberately mix two trigger modalities — not all event-driven:**
>     - **Event / file-landing** (ADF Storage-Event trigger / Airflow file-sensor or dataset DAG) —
>       fires when the Job drops a file. Use for high-frequency/latency-sensitive feeds (e.g. orders).
>     - **Schedule / time** (ADF Schedule or tumbling-window trigger / Airflow cron `schedule`) —
>       fires on a clock, processes whatever landed in the window. Use for daily/batch feeds
>       (e.g. inventory, feedback). **Set the load schedule to fire *after* the file-creation Job**
>       (e.g. Job drops inventory 01:00 → scheduled load 01:15) so there's data to pick up.
>     - _Why both:_ exercises both ADF/Airflow trigger types **and** DataQ's pipeline-run monitoring +
>       10-min polling / gap-recovery fallback (the poll only meaningfully bites on scheduled pipelines).
>   Flow C (flat-file in place) has no load step — the Job's file drop *is* the arrival; DataQ reads
>   in place (its own W5 schedule or arrival event).
>   Proposed split (adjustable): **orders → event**, **tracking → event**, **inventory → schedule**,
>   **feedback → schedule** — one event + one schedule per warehouse target (Snowflake & UC) for parity.
> - **Required by the Job runtime:** Container Apps Jobs are **ephemeral** → the generator's
>   state must move off local disk to **blob** (ADLS). `state.py` needs a pluggable backend
>   (local FS today; ADLS blob for the Job). See work items below.

- [x] **Generator `tick` mode** — `python -m mockdata tick --feed {orders,tracking,inventory,feedback}` emits **one** batch-suffixed file per dataset (`<name>_b000123.<fmt>`, append-style), not the full backfill. `init` subcommand seeds state + emits the reference loads once. ADLS impl coded, runnable against real ADLS post-apply

## 6. Performance / scale testing (post-v1 / backlog)

> Rides §2's parameterizable volume (`--seed` + a `--volume` knob): generate **S / M / L / XL** of the same
> datasets, run, measure, record. **Baseline-first** — establish numbers + a regression budget; no hard SLAs
> yet (set them once we know where the knees are). Standalone harness script; the **QA/QE agent (§4)** can
> invoke it later as a perf-smoke. Needs §2 (generators) + §3 (the run paths) green first.

**Lead axes (do these first):**
- [ ] **Run scaling** *(headline)* — GX run wall-clock vs **rows × #checks-per-suite**, per datasource (Snowflake · UC · flat-file CSV+Parquet). Capture throughput (rows/sec) + **worker memory peak** (GX pulls the batch in-memory) — and note whether time is in the warehouse query or GX itself.
- [ ] **Profiler latency** — profile a **wide** table (many columns) to quantify the known **N+1 per-column top-values** cost ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)) and the payoff once it's batched.
- [ ] **DB growth** — p95 latency of `/results`, `/dashboard/summary`, `/runs` as `results`/`runs` grow to 100k+ rows; validates the retention-sweep index ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)).

**Secondary axes:**
- [ ] **Concurrency / throughput** — N suites at once: Celery pool saturation, the 60s scheduler beat under many due schedules (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), run-now storms → queue wait time.
- [ ] **Flat-file batch resolution at scale** — resolving the latest batch among a large landing zone (thousands of files) — the list+regex cost.

**Method:**
- [ ] One script: param = datasource × volume tier × #checks; emits a CSV/JSON of (metric, value) per run
- [ ] Record a **baseline table** (commit it with the QA/QE agent in §4) + a **regression budget** (e.g. fail if a tier regresses > X% vs baseline) — so perf regressions are caught, not just measured once
- [ ] Capture both **app** metrics (run duration, profiler latency, API p95) and **resource** metrics (worker mem peak, DB CPU)
- [ ] Feed findings back: confirm/close #327 (profiler) + #323 (retention index); file new issues for any knee found

Rotation persists before the probe, so a bad new credential surfaces as 502 `connection_test_failed`; a store-write failure is 502 `connection_secret_write_failed` with the old credential untouched. 6 TestClient tests (rotate+verify ok, failed-verify-but-rotation-persists, write-fail 502, 404, secret-required 422). 129	- [x] ✅ API: CRUD for suites and GX expectations (Snowflake path) — **suites** (PR-B1): `suite_service` + `/suites` CRUD (`connection_id` validated then immutable; delete cascades to checks). **checks** (PR-B2): `check_service` + nested `/suites/{id}/checks` CRUD surfacing `kind` + `warn/fail/critical_threshold` + GX `expectation_type`/`config`. v1 monitor-kind guard (only `expectation`; reserved kinds → 422, ADR 0012); checks scoped to their suite (cross-suite access → 404); thresholds are `Decimal` in (exact `Numeric` storage) / `float` out (clean JSON). 24 TestClient tests; all four modules 100%. Share-based access filtering deferred to the suite-sharing task; **DQ-dimension classification** deferred + tracked ([#124](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/124))
130	- [x] ✅ API: suite sharing — assign users with owner / editor / viewer roles — **sharing API + authz core** (PR-E1): `suite_authz.require_permission` (404-hides a suite with no access, 403s an insufficient level) + `share_service` + `/suites/{id}/shares` CRUD. Schema vocab `view`/`edit`/`admin` + implicit owner=`created_by`; **admin can delete + manage shares** (per decision); grant-to-owner/unknown → 422; manage needs `admin`, list needs `view`. **Enforcement** (PR-E2): `require_permission` applied across the suite endpoints (GET=view, PATCH=edit, DELETE=admin) + all check endpoints (reads=view, writes=edit); `list_suites` scoped to owned-or-shared. Lands the access control deferred in B1/B2. ~26 TestClient tests across the matrix acting as different users (viewer reads-not-writes, editor writes-not-deletes, admin deletes, outsider→404, list scoping); shares/suites/checks routes + share_service all 100%
131	- [x] ✅ API: suite export to JSON + import from JSON — `suite_io_service` + `GET /suites/{id}/export` (view) / `POST /suites/import` (any authed user, like create). Document is **connection-agnostic** — omits all DB identity (`id`/`connection_id`/`created_by`/timestamps), so it's a reusable template; import **re-binds** to a freshly chosen `connection_id` and owns the new suite as the importer. Round-trippable: thresholds are `Decimal` in/out (exact). Import is **atomic** — every check kind is validated before any row is written (bad doc → 422, nothing persisted); unknown `version` → 422; missing connection → 422. Checks emitted in stable creation order (diffable). 148	- [x] ✅ Column profiler endpoint (Snowflake) — nulls, distinct count, min / max, top values — `profile_service` + `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (require_permission **edit**, suite-scoped so the connection is access-gated). Reads-only, persists nothing: one aggregate query (row count + null/distinct/min/max per column) + one top-N-values query per column, then `assemble_profile`. **SQL-injection-safe** — queries are built with the **SQLAlchemy Core expression language** (`select`/`table`/`column`, dialect-quoted) so there's no raw-string SQL sink (no S608/B608/CodeQL `py/sql-injection`); identifiers are additionally allowlist-validated (`validate_identifier`, strict `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_$]*$`) as defence-in-depth + a clean early 422, and `top_n` is `int()`-coerced. v1 → 422: non-Snowflake type (dispatch generalises Week 5), bad identifier, no schema; execution failure → 502 (adapter exception never echoed). min/max/top-values NaN-sanitised. 28 tests (22 pure: identifier allowlist incl. injection strings, compiled-SQL builders, assembly, div-by-zero, NaN; 6 endpoint via fake conn: stats, injection-422, unsupported-type-422, 502, edit-gated, no-schema-422); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 90% (live `_open_connection` is the deferred warehouse seam)
149	- [x] ✅ Column profiler endpoint (ADLS / S3) — same stats via Pandas on sampled file — extended `profile_service` into a type dispatcher (`profile_connection`): SQL types → in-warehouse aggregation (unchanged), flat-file types (`adls_gen2`/`s3`) → download a **sample** of the file into Pandas and compute the same stats (`profile_dataframe`). Same `POST /suites/{id}/profile` endpoint, now polymorphic: SQL targets pass `table`/`schema`, flat-file targets pass `path` (+ optional `file_format`, else inferred from extension); response carries whichever identity applies. CSV + Parquet. **pandas "load less data" levers** (per the user's scale.html prompt): column projection (CSV `usecols`, Parquet `columns=` via pyarrow schema) so profiling 3 of N columns doesn't read all N, + row sampling (`_SAMPLE_ROWS`), + **zero-copy Arrow-backed dtypes** on the Parquet read (`dtype_backend="pyarrow"` — parquet is already Arrow, kept zero-copy; the stat helpers + `_to_native` are Arrow-scalar/`pd.NA`-safe). Stats are vectorised pandas (no Python row loops — enhancingperf N/A; CoW is a no-op for a read-only profiler + a process-global flag, so not toggled; pyarrow CSV engine rejected — it's incompatible with `nrows`/callable-`usecols`). NaN/Timestamp→JSON-safe. v1 → 422: unsupported type (UC still pending), missing target, unknown format, missing column; read failure → 502 (exception never echoed). Reuses `ColumnProfile`/`ProfileResult`. 24 new tests (pure `profile_dataframe`/`infer_file_format`/`_to_native`/projection-parse + endpoint via fake `_read_dataframe`); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 85% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered — deferred smoke). _Deferred: streaming/range reads (whole object still downloaded before sampling) + out-of-core (Dask) — future work if profiling cost bites._

UC's **3-level namespace** (`catalog.schema.table`) is handled by building the table with a `quoted_name(f"{catalog}.{schema}", quote=False)` so the dialect emits three dotted parts rather than quoting the dotted string as one — verified by compiling the real builders against the Databricks dialect. Injection-safe: every part (catalog/schema/table/column) is allowlist-validated (no raw-string SQL, no CodeQL py/sql-injection surface). Endpoint gained `catalog` (request + response); the dispatcher requires it for UC (`422 profile_target_invalid` if absent). 12 new tests (catalog-aware builders compiled to SQL, `_engine_args` URL dispatch, UC endpoint stats via the same fake conn, missing-catalog 422, bad-catalog-identifier 422); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 86% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered — deferred smoke). **Completes the column profiler across all 4 datasources.**

Extracted the shared GX machinery — snake_case→GX-class translation, GX-result→`SuiteOutcome` mapping, and the suite/validation-definition `run_expectations` flow — into `datasources/gx_runner.py` (reused by the Snowflake runner and the future UC runner; no duplication). 154	- [x] ✅ Batch resolution — resolve batching regex to matched files, pick latest or specific batch — `resolve_batch` (`datasources/flatfile.py`): a batch `pattern` is a regex whose **first capture group is the batch key**; `latest` selects the greatest key (lexicographic — ISO dates sort chronologically), falling back to the storage modified-time when the pattern has no group; `specific` selects the file whose key equals a given `batch`. `BatchNotFoundError` on no match / unknown batch. The filter+select logic is pure and fully unit-tested (8 tests); object listing (`list_files` — S3 `list_objects_v2` paginated / ADLS `list_blobs`) is the live seam, and `resolve_batch_file` composes list+resolve. _Wiring a check's target to a resolved batch path is the Week-5 `run_suite` dispatch._

Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_
203	- [x] ✅ **Connections — `ConnectionDrawer` trimmed to edit-only** — create now lives on the page; the drawer drops the type/env Selects + secret + create branch, shows type/env read-only, and the list owns a simpler `editing: Connection | null` state. _(PR-A2)_
204	- [x] ✅ **Checks — suite selection as a route + categorized expectation picker** — `/suites/:suiteId` makes the selected suite deep-linkable and survives the check-editor round-trip; the catalog gains a `category` (Column values / Table shape) and the picker groups by it (antd optgroups). Reserved Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift categories (ADR 0012) land with the dedicated page. _(PR-B1)_
205	- [x] ✅ **Checks — dedicated `/suites/:suiteId/checks/new` page** — category → expectation → config/thresholds, with reserved monitor-kind categories (Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift, ADR 0012) shown disabled. Shared form logic extracted to `checkForm.ts` (conversions) + `checkFormFields.tsx` (field components), reused by the page and the now edit-only `CheckDrawer`. The natural home for the [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target table/path field. _(PR-B2; `CheckNew.tsx`)_
206	
207	**Week 4 total: 26 / 26 ✅ — complete** _(connection manager UI complete (6/6); check editor core shipped + **dry-run preview** (PR-D1) + **column profiler panel** (PR-D2) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** (PR #246 — closes the flat-file + Unity Catalog target inputs, making suites runnable); **check version-history drawer** (#280; backend #281 + frontend #282 — view-only, restore deferred to #283); **suite export/import UI** (PR-E1); **suite sharing panel** (PR-E2 backend + PR-E3 UI, gated on `SuiteRead.my_permission`); **GX-Cloud redesign complete (4/4)** — classified connection + check create pages, edit-only drawers, suite-route/categorized picker (PR-A1/A2/B1/B2); PR-3c polish 3/3 (Settings `extra="forbid"` shipped via the `.env`/`.env.app` split, #209); **Monaco custom-SQL editor** (ADR 0019 — custom-SQL as a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation`; backend #258 + frontend #259); **admin control centre** (#289 — workspace-admin authz + `/admin/{suites,users,access}` + the `/admin` page & 403, pulling the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward))_
208	
209	---
210	
211	## Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling
212	
213	**Exit gate:** Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.
214	
215	### Async execution backend (10 tasks — 10/10 ✅)
216	- [x] ✅ Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution — `run_suite` task + `run_service` — landed early via [PR 4a](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/74) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)
217	- [x] ✅ Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch — select the `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`), replacing the Snowflake-hardcoded wiring in `worker/tasks.py`; the seam that makes the flat-file / UC run paths route correctly and post-v1 RDBMS adapters a drop-in — [PR #146-fix](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/146) _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md); profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry in the same effort, [#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147))_
218	- [x] ✅ **Check target table/path** — modeled as a **per-suite** datasource-shaped `Suite.target` (JSONB, shaped like the column-profiler request: `table`/`schema`/`catalog`/`path`/`file_format`), since `execute_run` runs all of a suite's checks against one batch (the GX data-asset-per-suite shape). `services/run_target.resolve_target` resolves it per connection type to the runner's `(table, schema, catalog)` triple (flat-file path rides the table-shaped `CheckRunner`'s `table` slot); `validate_target` is the write-time 422 guard on suite create/update. Migration `b1f2c3d4e5a6` (additive nullable column, tested up/down on Postgres). **Dispatch ungated**: `_trigger_suites` now hands each created run to `run_suite.delay` (lazy import — worker↔service cycle), broker-fail marks the run `failed`; `run_suite` resolves the target from the suite (targetless → clean `failed`), so the worker no longer takes a `table` arg; stale `until_week3` wording removed. 13 resolver units + worker/orchestration/suite-API target tests. _(Re-tracked from Week 3; [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215); PR-C0a)_
219	- [x] ✅ **Manual run trigger + run/result read API** — `POST /suites/{id}/run` queues a `Run` and dispatches it via the shared `run_dispatch.dispatch_run` helper (**edit**-gated — the capability ladder grants "trigger runs" at edit); the suite target (#215) is resolved **up front** so a targetless/misconfigured suite fails fast with 422 instead of a queued→failed run, and a broker outage marks the run `failed` + returns 503 (same contract as the probe). New `api/v1/runs.py` read surface for the results page: `GET /runs?suite_id=&status=&limit=` and `GET /runs/{id}` (run + joined `Result`s) are **suite-scoped** — the list filters to accessible suites and the detail gates on `require_permission(view)`, so per-suite sharing + existence-hiding hold (exactly why direct-Postgres/Grafana is rejected as the primary surface); `GET /pipeline_runs?provider=&status=` is the orchestration monitoring feed (auth-only, not suite-scoped). Read helpers live in `run_service.list_runs/get_run/list_results` + `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs`. 14 DB-backed API tests (trigger happy/targetless-422/edit-403/no-access-404/broker-503; list scoping+filters+limit; detail+results / 404s; pipeline filters + auth). _(Run-enablement gap; PR-C0b; gates the Week-6 Results page PR-C1)_
220	- [x] ✅ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status — `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (suite-scoped): run lifecycle status + per-check resolution (status or null=pending) + a status histogram, the compact shape the live-progress UI polls. DB-driven (worker writes status + result rows); GX's atomic batch means per-check rows land at completion, so this reports lifecycle + final resolution (documented). **[PR #301](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/301)** (A1)
